tasks.intent:
Set up location and price filters for Miami area boat search. CRITICAL: Do NOT call done() in your first step - you must actually interact with the filter controls.

WHAT TO CLICK:
- Location/zip code input field - looks like a white text box with placeholder text like 'City, State or ZIP'
- Radius dropdown - typically says '25 miles' or 'Within X miles' near the location field
- Price filter controls - look for 'Min Price' or 'Price Range' section with dollar amount dropdowns
- Sort dropdown - usually in top right of results area, says 'Sort by:' followed by current selection

WHAT TO NEVER CLICK:
- Large boat photos in search results
- Social media icons (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter symbols)
- Dealer name links or logo images
- Heart/favorite star icons on listings
- Navigation menu dropdowns in header

STEPS:
1. Look for location input field and click() on it
2. Clear any existing text and type_text('33101')
3. Look for radius dropdown and click() to set it to 25 miles
4. Find price filter section and click() on minimum price dropdown
5. Select $35,000 minimum price option
6. Find sort dropdown (usually top right of results) and click() on it
7. Select 'Recently Updated' or 'Newest First' option
8. VERIFICATION: Read the result count on page - it should show filtered results
9. Do NOT call done() until you have actually clicked and typed in ALL filter controls

ERROR HANDLERS:
- Cookie popup appears → click() the Accept/OK button once
- Page loads slowly → wait 3 seconds, then proceed
- Filter dropdown doesn't respond → scroll(direction='up', amount=3) to ensure it's visible, then retry
- If result count still shows 1000+ listings, filters were NOT applied - retry filter steps

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
- Location should show '33101' or 'Miami' area
- Result count should be LESS than 500 listings (filtered)
- Sort should show 'Recently Updated' or similar
- FAILURE: If result count is still 1000+, filters failed

Extract boat listing data from search results. Process each listing by clicking the TITLE TEXT (never the photo) and gathering all available information.

WHAT TO CLICK:
- Listing title text - blue clickable text showing year, make, model BELOW the photo
- Back arrow or use Alt+Left to return to search results
- 'Next' pagination button at bottom of page when current page is complete

WHAT TO NEVER CLICK:
- Large boat photos - these open fullscreen gallery traps showing '1 of N'
- Heart/favorite icons on listing cards
- Dealer contact buttons or phone number links
- Social media sharing buttons
- Related/recommended boat suggestions

STEPS FOR EACH LISTING:
1. Find the blue title text (Year Make Model) BELOW a boat photo and click() on it
2. IMMEDIATELY after page loads, read the URL from browser address bar at top - this is MANDATORY
3. Scroll through the listing page to find Description and More Details sections
4. Extract: Year, Make, Model, Price (always required)
5. Look for phone number in contact section or description (optional)
6. Look for seller name in contact info (optional)
7. Use key_press(keys='alt+left') to go back to search results
8. Move to next listing on the page
9. When page is complete, click() 'Next' button to go to next page

ERROR HANDLERS:
- Photo gallery opens (shows '1 of N') → key_press(keys='Escape'), then key_press(keys='alt+left')
- 404 or listing unavailable → key_press(keys='alt+left'), continue to next listing
- Off-site navigation (dealer website) → key_press(keys='alt+left') immediately
- Popup ads → click() close button (X), continue extraction

DATA EXTRACTION RULES:
- URL from browser address bar is MANDATORY - read it immediately after detail page loads
- Extract partial data - do NOT skip listings without phone numbers
- A listing is viable with just Year/Make/Model/Price/URL
- Phone and seller name are OPTIONAL bonus fields

OUTPUT FORMAT:
For each listing, call done() with this exact structure:
{
  "success": true,
  "summary": "Year: 2019 | Make: Sea Ray | Model: Sundancer 320 | Price: $89,900 | URL: https://www.boattrader.com/listing/2019-sea-ray-sundancer-320-8234567 | Phone: 305-555-0123 | Seller: John Smith"
}

Do NOT output template placeholders like 'Year: <from title>' - use actual extracted values.
If phone/seller not found, omit those fields: 'Phone: Not listed | Seller: Not listed'

loop.pagination_intent: Click the 'Next' button at the bottom of search results page to load the next page of listings. Stop when no 'Next' button is visible.
